home.heading: 🧑‍🚀 Hello, Astronaut!
home.intro: Welcome to the official blog starter template for Astro. This template serves as a lightweight, minimally-styled starting point for anyone looking to build a personal website, blog, or portfolio with Astro.
home.integrations: This template comes with a few integrations already configured in your astro.config.mjs file. You can customize your setup with Astro Integrations to add tools like Tailwind, React, or Vue to your project.
home.getStarted: Here are a few ideas on how to get started with the template:---

tips.editHeader: Edit your site header items in src/components/Header.astro
tips.editFooter: Add your name to the footer in src/components/Footer.astro
tips.checkBlog: Check out the included blog posts in src/content/blog/
tips.customizeLayout: Customize the blog post page layout in src/layouts/BlogPost.astro

home.haveFun: Have fun! If you get stuck, remember to read the docs or join us on Discord to ask questions.
home.moreTemplates: Looking for a blog template with a little more personality? Check out astro-blog-template by Maxi Ferreira.

using-mdx.title: Using MDX
content: This theme comes with the @astrojs/mdx integration installed and configured in your astro.config.mjs config file. If you'd prefer not to use MDX, you can disable support by removing the integration from your config file.

MDX is a special kind of Markdown that supports embedded JavaScript and JSX syntax. This unlocks the ability to mix JavaScript and UI components into your Markdown content for things like charts or interactive alerts.
If you have existing content built in MDX, this integration will hopefully make migrating to Astro a breeze.

markdown-style-guide.title: Markdown Style Guide
markdown-style-guide.description: Here is a sample of some basic Markdown syntax that can be used when writing Markdown content in Astro.
content: Here is a sample of some basic Markdown syntax that can be used when writing Markdown content in Astro.
